opportunity_summary: Underground mine data center in Nordfjord, Norway. PUE 1.08-1.15 using fjord seawater cooling at 8C from 100m depth. Supports DLC, inline cooling, immersion, and CRAH. 80MW current capacity expanding to 200MW. 90MW cooling via 12x7.5MW heat exchangers. Up to 300kW/rack with DLC. Waste heat recovery program operational. 120,000 sqm whitespace potential.
confidence_reason: Official Lefdal Mine website confirms PUE 1.08-1.15, fjord seawater cooling, DLC and immersion support, 80MW capacity. ABB press release corroborates. Multiple industry sources confirm specs.
source_1_evidence_snippet: PUE 1.08-1.15 depending on cooling tech, UPS config, and scale. Fjord seawater at 8C from 100m depth. 12 heat exchangers at 7.5MW each = 90MW cooling. Supports fan walls, CRAH, inline, DLC, and immersion cooling.
source_2_evidence_snippet: 80MW capacity (expanded from 20MW in 2024). 120,000 sqm whitespace, up to 200MW. Up to 50kW/rack air-cooled, higher with DLC/immersion. 132kV grid connection.
notes: Underground mountain facility 700m inside mountain. Natural EMP protection. Fjord seawater cooling requires no additives. Waste heat program returns heated water to fjord or district use. 100% hydroelectric power. Modular build-out across 6 levels, 75 chambers.
heat_recovery_details: Heated seawater transported up via siphon effect, can be circulated back to fjord or used in waste heat program.
expansion_details: Can expand to 200MW total across 120,000 sqm whitespace. New cooling level with 200MW redundant capacity planned.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Increased available power from 20MW to 80MW. Possibility to expand to 200MW capacity.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.07 via 100% free-air cooling at 66N latitude (avg 1.3C). 4MW facility on 2.5 hectares, 6 data halls. Acquired by Northern Data AG (2021) rebranded as Ardent. 100% hydroelectric power. GPU/HPC focused. Ardent states liquid cooling planned for future builds. Pod-based 180kW system (18 racks). Up to 25kW/rack.
confidence_reason: PUE 1.07 confirmed by Hydro66 official site, Net Zero DC, and multiple industry sources. Capacity confirmed via datacentermap. Northern Data acquisition confirmed by BusinessWire. Liquid cooling is planned per Ardent/baxtel but not yet deployed at this site.
source_1_title: Boden with Hydro66 — Net Zero Data Center
source_1_evidence_snippet: PUE 1.07. ArcticPrime Colo. 180kW per pod (18 rack enclosures). Up to 25kW/rack. GPU and HPC workloads. 100% CFE. Starting EUR88/kW/month.
source_2_title: What is data center PUE? — Hydro66
source_2_evidence_snippet: PUE always less than 1.07. Power conditioning, UPS, air handling and efficient lighting add max 7% to core IT load.
notes: Originally launched 2015 as 2.3MW. Tripled to ~11MW then focused on 4MW colo. Acquired by Northern Data AG Oct 2021 for ~EUR25M. Rebranded Ardent Data Centers. Located next to Facebook/Meta Lulea campus area. Avg annual temp 1.3C. 80km south of Arctic Circle. Ardent brand states future facilities will use liquid cooling for HPC.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Fully operational since 2016. Six data center halls on 2.5 hectares.

opportunity_summary: Underground ex-military tunnel facility near Pori, Finland. 11MW, 8,500 sqm across 9 tunnel halls. Direct liquid cooling (Dell DLC3000) operational since late 2023, up to 80kW/rack. Natural 8C year-round bedrock cooling. 100% renewable energy + own solar plant. 0.5MW modular units. Supports AI/HPC workloads.
confidence_reason: Verne Global official site confirms DLC operational, 11MW, underground tunnels. DCD and TechHQ articles confirm Dell DLC3000 deployment with 80kW/rack. BusinessFinland confirms specs. PUE not publicly disclosed.
source_1_title: Verne Global Finland — Sustainable Data Center
source_1_evidence_snippet: 11MW overall site capacity. Direct liquid cooling integrated and fully operational. Underground ex-military tunnels. 9 tunnel halls. 100% renewable energy + own solar plant. AI-focused operations.
source_2_title: Verne Global adds liquid cooling to its underground Finnish data center
source_2_evidence_snippet: Dell DLC3000 solutions with 4th Gen Intel Xeon. Up to 80kW cooling per rack. Direct liquid cooling for high-density AI/HPC workloads.
notes: Originally created by Ficolo, acquired by Digital 9 Infrastructure in 2022, then merged into Verne Global brand. Tunnels quarried in 1960s by Finnish Defence Forces. Bedrock maintains 8C year-round. Modular 0.5MW units. Both shared and dedicated spaces available.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Overall site capacity 11MW. DLC fully operational.

opportunity_summary: Largest certified data center in Nordics. 24MW IT capacity across 15,000 sqm (34,000 sqm total). PUE target <1.2. Liquid cooling for AI/GPU loads (dedicated cooling options PDF). Gcore deployed 1MW+ GPU cluster (H100/A100). Waste heat recovery serves ~14,000 apartments via Helen district heating. 4MW expansion completed early 2025. 100% renewable energy.
confidence_reason: Telia official site confirms 24MW, PUE goal <1.2, heat recovery to 14,000 apartments. DCD confirms Gcore 1MW GPU cluster. Liquid cooling confirmed for AI/GPU loads but specific cooling tech details behind separate PDF. PUE is a target, not measured value.
source_1_evidence_snippet: IT Power Capacity 24MW. Data Hall Space 15,000 sqm. PUE goal <1.2. Waste heat to Helen district heating for ~14,000 apartments. Cooling Options for AI/GPU Loads available.
source_2_title: Gcore to deploy AI GPU cloud infrastructure in Telia's Helsinki data center
source_2_evidence_snippet: Gcore deploying 1MW+ GPU cluster. Powered by renewable energy. Excess heat reused for heating apartments.
notes: Opened 2018. Largest certified open data center in Nordics. Heat recovery priority: 1) heat recovery, 2) free cooling, 3) mechanical cooling. Connected to Helen district heating network. Gcore contract signed and deployed within 1 month. Flexible kW-based pricing.
heat_recovery_details: Waste heat transferred to Helen's district heating network, serving approximately 14,000 apartments in Helsinki area. Heat pumps take 30C return water and return 20C water to DC loop.
expansion_details: 4MW capacity addition completed early 2025 to meet AI demand.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Building 4MW more capacity, completed early 2025. AI demand driving expansion.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.10-1.15. Liquid cooling ready since 2023: in-row CDUs, in-rack CDUs, direct-to-chip, rear-door, immersion. Over 100kW/rack supported. District heating connected for waste heat. 100% renewable wind power. OCP Ready certified. Expanding from 24MW to 28MW (Jan 2026). Built 2024.
confidence_reason: Baxtel confirms PUE 1.15, built 2024. Conapto official site confirms liquid cooling support over 100kW/rack, district heating, OCP Ready. DCD confirms 20MW campus. Expansion to 28MW confirmed by baxtel news.
source_1_title: Conapto opens Stockholm 4 South — A New, Sustainable Data Center
source_1_evidence_snippet: Officially opened Aug 30 2024. High density and liquid-cooled deployments. District heating connected. Fossil free 100% renewable electricity. Flexible design for customer-tailored solutions.
source_2_evidence_snippet: All new capacity since 2023 supports liquid-cooled deployments. Over 100kW/rack. In-row CDUs or in-rack CDUs. Direct-to-chip, rear-door, immersion cooling options.
notes: Part of Stockholm South Campus with Stockholm 2 South. Combined 5,200 sqm computer rooms. 2 building floors. OCP Ready for Hyperscale certified. 100% renewable wind power for 10+ years. Utility provider: Ellevio.
heat_recovery_details: Connected to district heating network for excess heat recycling to warm homes in Stockholm area.
expansion_details: Adding 8MW to take site to 28MW, effective January 2026.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Conapto to Add 8MW to Stockholm Data Center. Taking the site to 28MW effective January 2026.

opportunity_summary: 5MW facility in Stockholm Kista using proprietary fluid-based cooling from sister company GoGreenHost. Up to 100kW/rack. Waste heat at 110C for district heating via Fortum Varme — could heat 10,000 apartments. Part of Stockholm Data Parks initiative. Authorized to serve Swedish government IT. Current operational status unconfirmed.
confidence_reason: DCD and Stockholm Data Parks confirmed 5MW plan, fluid-based cooling, 100kW/rack, heat recovery in 2017. Sweden Database 2025 lists Borderlight as active. But no recent confirmation of operational status found. GoGreenHost partnership for specialized blades confirmed.
source_1_title: Borderlight set to build 5MW data center in Stockholm, sell waste heat
source_1_evidence_snippet: 5MW data center in Kista suburb. Fluid-based cooling system (details secret). GoGreenHost specialized server blades up to 100kW/rack. Waste heat at 110C for Fortum Varme district heating. Could heat 10,000 apartments.
source_2_title: New high-density data center heats 10,000 households in Stockholm
source_2_evidence_snippet: Borderlight partnership with Stockholm city, Fortum Varme, Ellevio AB and AB Stokab. Close to 100% heat recovery target.
notes: Borderlight established 2001. One of 5 IT companies authorized for Swedish government telecom services. GoGreenHost sister company provides proprietary high-temp server hardware. Part of Stockholm Data Parks government initiative. Operational status needs direct verification.
heat_recovery_details: GoGreenHost hardware produces heat at up to 110C, meeting district heating plant quality requirements. Connected to Fortum Varme network. Target: close to 100% recovery of consumed electrical power.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: CEO expected operation within 6-12 months of Aug 2017 announcement. Current status unverified.

full_address: Building A101, Cody Park South Gate, Old Ively Road, Farnborough, GU14 0LH
opportunity_summary: PUE 1.14 across 36-acre campus. 51MW IT load across 6 modular data centers. Hybrid cooling: free-air, evaporative (indirect), plus liquid cooling 'liquid optimisers'. 100% renewable energy + HVO backup generators. Rainwater harvesting for cooling. Tier III resilience. Operational since 2011. Synchronous replication to London.
confidence_reason: Ark official site confirms 51MW, Cody Park campus, liquid optimisers. PUE 1.14 confirmed by datacenters.com and Colo-X. Multiple sources corroborate. Address and coordinates confirmed by Datanet hosting partner.
source_1_evidence_snippet: 51MW compute capacity across 6 modular data centers. Free-air, evaporative, and liquid cooling. Four 33kV grid connections from two diverse substations. N+1 grid feeds.
source_2_title: Ark Data Centres Cody Park A101
source_2_evidence_snippet: Typical campus PUE approximately 1.14. Liquid optimisers improve thermal efficiency. Off-site modular construction.
notes: Flagship campus. Operational since 2011. Tier III resilience. 100% renewable energy. HVO (Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil) backup generators. Rainwater harvesting for cooling water. Enterprise and government clients. Synchronous replication to London.

full_address: Western International Park, Hayes Road, Southall, UB2 5XJ
opportunity_summary: PUE below 1.2 via fresh-air evaporative cooling. First London DC to deploy this cooling tech. 12MW total client power, 60,000 sqft. AI-Ready: supports direct-to-chip, immersive, and rear-door heat exchange liquid cooling within same workload. 6 data halls with flexible subdivision. Liquid cooled customer racks since 2015.
confidence_reason: Virtus official site confirms AI-Ready liquid cooling capabilities (DTC, immersive, RDHX). Colo-X confirms PUE <1.2 and 12MW. DCD corroborates. Virtus has been offering liquid cooling since 2015 per official video.
source_1_title: AI Ready Data Centres: Liquid Cooling
source_1_evidence_snippet: Multiple types of liquid and air cooling (including direct to chip) within same networking environment. First liquid cooled customer racks deployed in 2015.
source_2_evidence_snippet: 12MW total client power. 60,000 sqft net tech space. Fresh-air evaporative cooling. PUE below 1.2. Six data halls.
notes: Opened Q2 2015. Part of VIRTUS portfolio (29 data centers). Macquarie Asset Management owns VIRTUS. AI-Ready DCs allow DTC and air cooling in same workload. Flexible from single cabinet to full data hall.

full_address: Crossways Business Park, 11 Newton's Court, DA2 6QL, Dartford, United Kingdom
opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 via ultra-free air cooling. 10MW capacity, 800 racks across 6 data halls. 30,000 sqft. Aqua EcoPro+ free cooling chiller units (3x500kW). source_2_evidence_snippet: PUE 1.2 via ultra-free air cooling. 3x500kW Aqua EcoPro+ optimised free cooling chiller units. 12 custom cooling coils.

opportunity_summary: DOES NOT QUALIFY. Underground nuclear bunker DC in Sodermalm, Stockholm. 30m below granite. Free-air and mechanical cooling. Heat recovery: 2 heat pumps supply 70m pipe to district heating. Saves 275 MWh/year. Iconic facility but no liquid cooling, no published PUE, no confirmed MW capacity. Included for reference only.

source_2_evidence_snippet: Theoretical PUE below 1.0 when counting heat recovery credit. Saves 275 MWh/year and EUR40,000 annual electricity.
notes: EXCLUDED FROM QUALIFYING SET — included for completeness. Iconic underground DC in former nuclear bunker. 40cm thick door. WikiLeaks was a former customer. Heat recovery operational via 2 heat pumps. No liquid cooling deployed. No verifiable PUE or MW.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.09 via seawater cooling (first in world for DC use, since 2009). Standard PUE 1.2 with chilled water, 1.09-1.1 with seawater. Saves USD 1M annually. Cooling-free over half the year. Part of Stockholm Data Parks. No IT liquid cooling (DTC/immersion) currently confirmed.
confidence_reason: Data Center Knowledge article confirms PUE 1.09 with seawater, PUE 1.2 with chilled water, USD 1M annual savings. DCD confirms Stockholm Data Parks build. But no IT liquid cooling (DTC/immersion) evidence. MW capacity not publicly available.
source_1_title: Interxion Uses Sea Water to Cool Stockholm Data Centers
source_1_evidence_snippet: PUE 1.09 with seawater cooling. PUE 1.2 with chilled water. One of first seawater cooling systems for DC use (Jan 2009). Saves USD 1M annually. Sub-12-month payback.
notes: Seawater cooling pioneered here in 2009. Runs cooling-free over half the year. Part of Stockholm Data Parks initiative (EUR 29M facility planned). Note: seawater cooling is facility-level, not IT-level liquid cooling (DTC/immersion). Digital Realty acquired Interxion in 2020.
expansion_details: EUR 29M new facility planned as part of Stockholm Data Parks.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Interxion set to build EUR 29M facility with Stockholm Data Parks.

build_phase_if_known: 50MW operational, expanding to 70MW with 2 new buildings (liquid cooling ready)
opportunity_summary: 50MW operational (15,000 sqm / 129,000 sqft). Expanding to 70MW with 2 NEW buildings designed for liquid cooling (Feb 2025 announcement). Indirect free air cooling up to 35-40C. Heat recovery: 1.3MW heat per 1MW IT via heat pumps (25-30C to 90C). District heating connected. 100% renewable energy + own solar. Tier III equivalent. Land acquired from Onvest for expansion.
confidence_reason: DCD confirms Feb 2025 expansion announcement: 2 new buildings with liquid cooling, campus to 70MW. Verne Global official site confirms 50MW, heat recovery, renewable energy. Baxtel confirms address and coordinates. Computer Weekly confirms heat recovery 1.3MW/1MW ratio.
source_1_title: Verne to expand Helsinki data center campus with two buildings
source_1_evidence_snippet: Two new buildings will support liquid cooling. Campus to potential 70MW at full build-out. Currently 50MW across 15,000 sqm. Designed for AI, HPC and compute-intensive workloads.
source_2_title: Verne expands Helsinki data center campus with strategic site acquisition
source_2_evidence_snippet: Land purchased from Onvest. All new buildings designed to support liquid cooling. Enhanced efficiency and heat reuse.
notes: Opened Q2 2020 as Ficolo 'The Air'. Acquired by Digital 9/Verne Global. Tier III equivalent. Combined energy and cooling cells (CECC) with heat exchange wheel free cooling. Indirect free air cooling works up to 35-40C. Expansion is HIGH VALUE LEAD: new buildings being designed with liquid cooling from scratch.
heat_recovery_details: For each 1MW IT power, produces 1.3MW heat. Heat pumps use 300kW electricity to raise temperature from 25-30C to ~90C. Connected to local district heating network.
expansion_details: Two new buildings with liquid cooling support. Campus to 70MW total. Land acquired from Onvest.

facility_name: Boden Type Data Center One (BTDC One)
operator_company: Boden Type DC / Boden Business Agency
opportunity_summary: WORLD-RECORD PUE: Category 2 PUE 1.025 (instantaneous <1.01). Pod 1 (OCP CPUs) PUE <1.03, Pod 3 (ASICs) PUE <1.003. 600kW total, 180 sqm whitespace. EU H2020 prototype. Patented EcoCooling evaporative system — no liquid cooling. ASHRAE compliant at -30C with <100W overhead for 140kW IT load. Hive Digital partnership.
confidence_reason: BTDC official site confirms PUE 1.025 category 2, <1.003 per pod. DCD confirms EU H2020 funding, Feb 2019 inauguration, 600kW. EcoCooling confirms patented system. Data Centre Magazine confirms Hive partnership.

opportunity_summary: PUE 1.2 design target. 55MW compute capacity across 6 modular DCs. Mix of direct air, indirect air, and liquid cooling. SQ17 building: 24 data halls at 0.5MW each (12MW total). P1-P3: 6 data rooms at 760kW each (182 cabinets at 4.2kW). Multiple 33kV feeds. 100% renewable energy. Operational since 2010. Supports AI/high-density workloads.
